original_hypothesis.title: GaugeManager.createGauge can revert/bricked if bribefactory not configured (permissionless call as DoS/noise vector)
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: GaugeManager._deployBribes calls IBribeFactory(bribefactory).createBribe(...) but bribefactory is not set in initialize() and can be unset/zero. Permissionless createGauge/createGauges allow anyone to trigger attempts to create gauges; if bribefactory is misconfigured, these calls revert, enabling repeated revert spam and preventing legitimate gauge creation until configuration is fixed.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is primarily an operational/config risk but grounded in code: critical dependency bribefactory is not initialized. It can lead to bricked gauge creation and noisy reverted transactions that may interfere with automation or monitoring.
deployment_assumptions: bribefactory is not set or set incorrectly at some point (e.g., after upgrade/reset)
Protocol expects permissionless gauge creation to work continuously
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: GaugeManager.initialize() does not set bribefactory; only setBribeFactory() exists. No deployment script shown calling setBribeFactory for GaugeManager.

proof_of_signal.pattern: _deployBribes uses bribefactory; bribefactory not set in initialize()

preconditions: bribefactory == address(0) or points to non-contract/incorrect contract
Users attempt to create gauges (permissionless path)

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: gauge creation reverts until admin sets bribefactory
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No require(bribefactory != 0) before attempting bribe deployment; no staged initialization guard to block createGauge until configured

analysis_questions: Do deployment flows set bribefactory on GaugeManager before enabling public usage?
Is there a possibility bribefactory can be reset to 0 through upgrades/storage collisions?
validation_requirements: Review deployment scripts for setBribeFactory call (not present in provided scripts)
Simulate createGauge with bribefactory unset to confirm revert path and error messages

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: If operators accidentally deploy/upgrade GaugeManager without setting bribefactory, anyone calling createGauge will revert. Attackers can spam reverted createGauge transactions to create operational noise while gauge creation is unavailable.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: No direct fund loss; blocks onboarding of new gauges and associated incentives
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: LPs and voters for new pools; protocol growth

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No initialization completeness tests ensuring bribefactory is set before allowing gauge creation

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Monitor reverted createGauge calls and bribefactory==0 state

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
